questions.question: A bookshelf has $48$ books. If they are packed in boxes that hold $8$ books each, how many boxes are needed with $0$ leftover books?
What is the remainder when $27$ is divided by $9$ using the division algorithm?
If a bakery sells $240$ loaves of bread per day, and they are packed $12$ loaves per box, how many boxes are sold per day?
A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have $48$ pieces of candy and there are $8$ friends, how many pieces will each friend get if they use the division algorithm?
What is the remainder when $75$ is divided by $5$?
A water tank can hold $180$ gallons of water. If $12$ gallons of water are added to it every hour, how many hours will it take to fill the tank if it is currently empty?
What is the result of $24 div 3$ using the division algorithm?
A person has $120$ minutes to complete a task. If the task requires $8$ minutes of work and $2$ minutes of rest per cycle, how many complete cycles can the person complete?

In a game, a player scores $3$ points for every $5$ minutes played. If the player played for $35$ minutes, how many points will the player score? Show all your work and express your answer as a division result with remainder.

A library has $288$ books to put on shelves that can hold $16$ books each. How many shelves will the library need, and how many books will be left over? Use the division algorithm to solve the problem and show all your work.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to use real-life examples to understand the division algorithm.
Tip 2: Remind students to check their work by multiplying the quotient by the divisor and adding the remainder.
Tip 3: Suggest that students use visual aids like diagrams or charts to help with long division problems.
